research_question: Can we keep the calibrated v7 operational contract fixed, but replace the momentum-only score family with one PIT-based quality/profitability family, so that the resulting cross-sectional signal has thicker gross edge and better fixed-test robustness?

feature_set.interpretation: higher quarterly ex-nonrecurring ROE is better

score_family_contract.score_rule: mean(percentile_rank(roe_dt DESC), percentile_rank(roa_yearly DESC), percentile_rank(q_roe DESC), percentile_rank(q_dt_roe DESC)); require min_feature_count >= 3
score_family_contract.missing_data_rule: Only score rows with at least 3 non-null ranked features; otherwise emit null model_score_D0 and preserve the row for audit.

constants_frozen.selection_contract: same calibrated v7 0.70 ranking liquidity guard and same TopK=10 width
constants_frozen.portfolio_contract: same equal-weight daily Top10 extraction used by calibrated v7

disallowed_changes: Do not change the calibrated v7 liquidity guard threshold or guard stage.
Do not change benchmark or cost contracts.
Do not combine this round with weight-penalty, extraction, or turnover-suppression changes.
Do not sweep multiple quality/profitability feature sets inside this round.
Do not sweep multiple score aggregation formulas inside this round.
Do not mix value, investment, or momentum features into this family scaffold round.
notes: This round changes exactly one core dimension: the score family.
The purpose is to test whether a truly different PIT-based fundamental family can produce a stronger edge than the exhausted baseline momentum family.
This registration does not claim implementation completeness; it freezes the family definition and governance boundary first.
